description: Container widths on the Campus page feel cramped — the body copy blocks that are left-aligned (the 'More than a WeWork' anecdote, the 'What's it like' section, the bio cluster, etc.) sit inside max-w-3xl which is constraining them more than necessary at md+ viewports. Combined with the outer px-6 md:px-[4.5%] padding on the section wrappers, the readable column ends up narrower than feels right. Scope: (1) investigate which constraint dominates at 375/768/1024/1280px — likely some combination of max-w-3xl on content blocks, max-w-7xl on section wrappers, and px-[4.5%] outer padding; (2) define a sitewide canonical max content width for left-aligned body prose (options: max-w-4xl, max-w-5xl, or custom) and codify in DESIGN.md; (3) audit + apply across Campus, Story, Lab, NLA, Political Club, Visit, Events, People, Protocol, Home — anywhere max-w-3xl is used on a left-aligned body block; (4) reduce section-wrapper side padding if that's also part of the issue. Out of scope: centered hero text widths (those follow a different rule). Mobile-first: verify 375px still reads correctly. Acceptance: consistent left-aligned body container width across all pages; canonical 'body container max-width' rule in DESIGN.md; no 375px regression. Slot in after FRAC-25 Campus apply lands — separate PR from typography/color migrations.
title: Reduce side margins and expand left-aligned body containers on Campus (likely sitewide)
